# Cover And Art

## Priority Rule

Cover first.
Interior ornament second.
Deluxe art later.

## Minimum Required Art Pack

For a basic release, prepare:

- ebook cover
- print cover if releasing print
- title treatment
- author name treatment
- thumbnail-readability check

## Art Brief Fields

Capture these fields before generating:

- title
- subtitle
- author
- series status
- target platform
- target aspect ratio
- trim size if print
- tone keywords
- world motifs
- must-include items
- must-avoid items
- typography direction

## Aethermoor And Six Tongues Direction

Use motifs such as:

- raven silhouettes
- wingscroll fragments
- roots, flowers, orchard details
- crystal light
- witness rings
- six-tongue geometry
- one subtle broken or unfinished seventh mark

Avoid:

- generic neon hacker UI as the main signal
- laptop-centric portal art as the main cover
- overbusy rune rings that fail at thumbnail size
- decorative density that overwhelms title readability

## Cover Decision Rule

If a concept works as a wallpaper or promo image but fails as a thumbnail, it is not the cover yet.

## Interior Art Rule

Little birds, flowers, borders, and chapter ornaments are good optional additions.
Do them after text lock unless the user explicitly prioritizes deluxe presentation.
